title: Agents dispatch lugs to spokes — humans must not relay instructions between agents
summary: When an AI agent identifies work for another spoke, it must write a task lug to that spoke's inbox directly. Asking the human to relay instructions between agents is an anti-pattern — the human becomes a message bus. Correct pattern: agent → lug → spoke inbox → autonomous pickup.
rule: Agent-to-agent communication goes through lug dispatch, not human relay.
why_it_matters: Surfaces the human as a bottleneck and breaks the autonomous execution loop. Discovered during COO Guide prompt iteration in S78.
